title: Contestability Primitive v1
description: Schema for P31 Contestability — allows affected parties to flag an AI decision for human review, suspending the decision's effects until review completes. Enforces D6 (CONTESTABILITY): affected parties can flag AI decisions for human review. Requires evidence or justification for the contest, not just a bare flag.

decision_id.description: Identifier of the AI decision being contested.

contest_reason.description: Non-empty justification for the contest. D6 requires evidence or justification, not a bare flag.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence supporting the contest (logs, outputs, affected party statements).

affected_party_id.description: Identifier of the affected party requesting review.

impact_description.description: Description of the impact on the affected party.

contest_evidence.description: Evidence gate: proof that the contest has substantive basis.

contest_status.description: Status of the contest. 'flagged' suspends decision effects. 'under_review' means human review is in progress. 'upheld' confirms the decision. 'overturned' reverses the decision. 'withdrawn' means the contesting party withdrew.

review.description: Human review record. Required when contest_status is 'upheld' or 'overturned'.

flagged_by.description: Identity of the party flagging the decision for review.

delegation_ref.description: Optional reference to a delegation token authorizing the flag.

authority.description: Authority gate: who is flagging the decision.

receipt_hash.description: Hash-chained proof of the contest declaration.

receipt.description: Receipt gate: hash-chained proof of contest.
